metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether docs/llm-framework.md currently contains any statement about projects adding code-review criteria via the overlay; if it does not, update the Context / Problem to accurately describe the full gap (both the 'projects may add criteria' baseline AND the runtime contract are absent from that file).
evidence: Full read of docs/llm-framework.md: zero occurrences of 'project', 'overlay', 'plan-review criteria', or 'code-review criteria'.

The content about projects adding code-review criteria through the overlay lives in docs/adr/0017-unified-criteria-registry.md ('Code-review gains project-supplied criteria through the same .rebar/criteria_routing.json overlay, on its code_review gate key').
ABSENCE: docs/llm-framework.md search for 'criteria' returns no matches.
findings.finding: The plan states docs/llm-framework.md 'says projects may add code-review criteria through the same overlay as plan-review,' but no such statement exists in that file — making the premise of the Context / Problem section unverifiable.
findings.location: Context / Problem section: 'it does not document the runtime contract for how a project code-review criterion flows through the pipeline'
scenarios: The plan's premise ('llm-framework.md says X but does not document Y') is false if llm-framework.md does not say X either — both the existing statement and the missing contract are absent, meaning the section fills a larger gap than described.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify the ticket id `rebar:41e2-a0a2-2db4-4847` is correct and resolvable; update the dependency reference if the ticket has been renamed, merged, or superseded.
evidence: show_ticket('rebar:41e2-a0a2-2db4-4847') returned: 'Ticket rebar:41e2-a0a2-2db4-4847 not found'.
No search hits for '41e2-a0a2-2db4-4847' in docs/ tree.
findings.finding: The plan declares a dependency on 'the CLI-admission child [rebar:41e2-a0a2-2db4-4847]' but that ticket was not found in the tracker, making the dependency chain unverifiable.
findings.location: Context / Problem: 'It depends on the CLI-admission child [rebar:41e2-a0a2-2db4-4847]'
scenarios: If the CLI-admission child ticket does not exist or has been renamed/merged, the plan's dependency claim is dangling — the 'completed seam' this doc ticket is meant to document may not be formally tracked, or the ticket id is stale.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the code-review arm of `criteria eval` in this plan (or in the dependency ticket): specify (a) the physical prompt-id mapping for a code-review project criterion (e.g. `project.foo` → `code-review-project-foo`), (b) how `calibrate_criterion` / the CLI dispatcher branches between plan-review and code-review gates, and (c) what error is raised when no eval spec exists for that physical id — so the `#### criteria eval resolution` subheading can be written as a normative contract.
evidence: Plan text: 'project code-review ids resolve and calibrate through the code-review arm of `criteria eval`'
src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py:414–420: `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id)` uses `PLAN_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX` unconditionally; no gate dispatch
src/rebar/_cli/_llm_eval_commands.py:144–155: preflight check calls `plan_review.registry.by_id(repo_root)` — no code-review branch
src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py:31: `PLAN_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX = 'plan-review-'` hardcoded with no gate-keyed variant
Ticket rebar:41e2-a0a2-2db4-4847 (the stated dependency that delivers this end-state) not found in the tracker
findings.finding: Aspect 3 ('#### criteria eval resolution') references a 'code-review arm of `criteria eval`' as the resolution/calibration path for project code-review ids, but this processing protocol is never defined in the plan, and inspection of the current codebase shows no such arm exists: `calibrate_criterion` (eval.py:384–421) calls `criterion_prompt_id` which hardcodes `PLAN_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX = 'plan-review-'`, and the CLI dispatcher (eval_commands.py:144–155) validates against `plan_review.registry.by_id` only. The physical prompt-id mapping convention for a code-review project criterion (the analog of `plan-review-project-<name>.md`) is never specified, so an author writing this subheading cannot describe the actual resolution rules.
findings.location: Approach section, Aspect 3 / `#### criteria eval resolution` subheading
scenarios: Author writing the `#### criteria eval resolution` subheading has no specification for what 'the code-review arm' does — what physical prompt id a `project.foo` code-review criterion resolves to, how `calibrate_criterion` routes between gates, or what error surfaces when no eval spec exists for that physical id.
Reviewer checking the resulting documentation against the delivered seam cannot verify correctness because the protocol is underspecified both in this plan and in the referenced dependency.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Protocol detail for Aspect 3' note to the Approach, e.g.: 'A code-review project criterion `project.<name>` maps to physical prompt id `code-review-project-<name>` (the code-review gate analog of `plan-review-project-<name>`). `calibrate_criterion` dispatches to the code-review arm when the id is active in `code_review.registry.effective_criteria(repo_root)`; it resolves the fixture at `.rebar/evals/code-review-project-<name>.eval.yaml`; a missing fixture raises a located `EvalError`.'

findings.finding: Approach aspect 3 claims 'project code-review ids resolve and calibrate through the code-review arm' of `criteria eval`, but the actual implementation (`src/rebar/_cli/_llm_eval_commands.py` `_criteria_eval`, lines 144–155) validates criterion ids exclusively against `plan_review.registry.by_id()`, and `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py:criterion_prompt_id()` unconditionally prefixes all ids with `plan-review-`. There is no code-review arm: a project code-review criterion passed to `rebar criteria eval` would be rejected as 'unknown criterion' by the plan-review registry lookup.

The plan's Aspect 3 ('`criteria eval` resolution — project code-review ids resolve and calibrate through the code-review arm') references a dispatch/routing protocol that the existing `criteria eval` code does NOT implement for `project.*` code-review criteria: `_llm_eval_commands.py:147` gates on `plan_review.registry.by_id()`, which will not contain a `project.*` id activated only under the `code_review` gate key, causing it to be rejected or fall through to `ValueError: no eval solver for prompt`. The plan claims this is 'purely mechanical documentation' with 'no new testable behavior', yet the behavior it would document (project code-review ids resolve via the code-review arm) is absent from the running code — creating a false contract.
findings.location: Approach section, Aspect 3; Acceptance Criteria AC line 1 marker 'criteria eval'; src/rebar/_cli/_llm_eval_commands.py:147 and src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py:187–280
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) verify there is a code path this analysis missed and cite it in the plan, or (b) narrow Aspect 3's documented claim to 'project code-review ids resolve through the code-review overlay runner in the gate workflow' (which IS implemented) and distinguish that from the `criteria eval` calibration path, noting calibration of project code-review criteria is a separate, deferred capability.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the upstream seam children actually deliver a mechanism to fan activated project.<name> criteria into the round_a/round_b finder passes (i.e., that the gate YAML or batch runner gains a dynamic injection seam); if not, revise Aspect 1 to accurately describe how project criteria flow (e.g., through effective_routing for Pass-3 threshold/blocking resolution rather than as round_a/round_b finders).
evidence: Plan text: 'activated project criteria are fanned into the finder passes (round_a/round_b) and each finding carries its logical criterion id project.<name>'

src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/code-review.yaml lines 77-95 and 109-127: round_a and round_b batch criteria are a closed static list of named overlay prompts; no dynamic extension seam for project.* ids

src/rebar/llm/code_review/registry.py lines 164-184: project criteria enter via effective_criteria()/effective_routing() (gate_key='code_review'), which is a Pass-3 routing mechanism, not a finder-fan-in to round_a/round_b
ABSENCE: no seam in the batch runner or gate YAML that reads effective_criteria() to extend the batch step criteria lists at runtime
findings.finding: Aspect 1 ('Round A/B fan-in') states that 'activated project criteria are fanned into the finder passes (round_a/round_b)' — but the code-review gate's round_a and round_b batch steps are closed, static lists of named overlay prompts (code-review-security, code-review-performance, etc.) with no dynamic injection point for project.<name> criteria. Project criteria enter the gate through effective_criteria()/effective_routing() for Pass-3 threshold resolution, not as finder passes in round_a/round_b. If no upstream seam child adds a structural injection mechanism into those batch steps, the documented 'fan-in to round_a/round_b' contract would misdescribe how project criteria actually flow through the pipeline.
findings.location: Approach section, Aspect 1: '#### Round A/B fan-in'
scenarios: If the documented contract says project criteria fan into round_a/round_b finders but they actually only affect Pass-3 routing, the doc would mis-specify the contract — exactly the silent-regression risk the plan is designed to prevent

A future implementer trying to add a project code-review criterion following the documented contract would expect their criterion to appear as a finder pass; instead it would only influence Pass-3 thresholds

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `'round_b'` to the AC2 markers list so both finder passes named in the Approach are verified present in the section body.
evidence: Approach item 1: "fanned into the finder passes (`round_a`/`round_b`)"
AC2 markers list: "'round_a'" — 'round_b' absent from the markers checked
findings.finding: AC2's verify script checks for 'round_a' as the content marker for the Round A/B fan-in aspect, but the Approach text specifies the section should document both 'round_a' and 'round_b'. An implementation that mentions only 'round_a' — omitting 'round_b' — would satisfy AC2 while leaving half the described deliverable unverified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — second bullet (AC2 verify script) vs. Approach section item 1
scenarios: An implementer writes the Round A/B fan-in subheading mentioning only 'round_a' passes'; AC2 exits 0 even though 'round_b' is entirely absent from the documented contract.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `'round_b'` to the markers list in AC2: `markers=['round_a','round_b','logical criterion id','effective routing','criteria eval','configuration error']`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `'round_b'` to the AC2 marker-token list so the verification script asserts both finder passes are documented in the fan-in subheading body.
evidence: Approach text: 'activated project criteria are fanned into the finder passes (`round_a`/`round_b`) and each finding carries its logical criterion id'
AC2 markers list: `['round_a','logical criterion id','effective routing','criteria eval','configuration error']` — `round_b` is absent
A section body containing only `round_a` would pass the AC grep while silently omitting the `round_b` pass from the documented contract
findings.finding: The approach description requires both `round_a` AND `round_b` to appear in the fan-in subheading body, but the AC marker-token verification script only checks for `'round_a'` — `round_b` is absent from the markers list. A section body that names `round_a` but omits `round_b` would pass the AC verification even though the contract is supposed to cover both finder passes.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2 marker-token verification script; Approach section — subheading 1 description
scenarios: Author writes the fan-in subheading body mentioning only `round_a` for brevity; the AC verification passes; `round_b`'s fan-in behavior is undocumented, leaving the contract incomplete for the second finder pass.
findings.suggested_fix: In AC2, change the markers list from `['round_a','logical criterion id','effective routing','criteria eval','configuration error']` to `['round_a','round_b','logical criterion id','effective routing','criteria eval','configuration error']`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether the 'four-pass contract' authoritative home is `docs/llm-framework.md` or `docs/review-kernel.md`, and update the location rationale and rejected-alternative accordingly — as written, the claim 'the four-pass contract already lives in llm-framework.md' is refuted by reading that file.
evidence: Full read of docs/llm-framework.md (484 lines): zero occurrences of 'four-pass', 'Pass-1', 'Pass-2', 'Pass-3', 'Pass-4', 'project criteria', 'overlay', or 'code-review criteria'.
docs/review-kernel.md opens: 'The review kernel — the shared four-pass review framework' — the four-pass contract lives here, not in llm-framework.md.
Plan's rejected-alternative rationale ('the four-pass contract already lives in llm-framework.md; splitting elsewhere would fragment the single authoritative reference') rests on this false premise.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'docs/llm-framework.md documents the four-pass contract' as the justification for adding the new section there, but a full read of docs/llm-framework.md shows it contains no mention of the four-pass review contract, Pass-1/2/3/4, or project criteria — those topics live in docs/review-kernel.md and docs/plan-review-gate.md.
findings.location: Context / Problem section: 'docs/llm-framework.md documents the four-pass contract'; Rejected alternative: 'the four-pass contract already lives in llm-framework.md'
scenarios: If a reviewer or implementer accepts the plan's location rationale at face value, they may not notice that the four-pass contract documentation is fragmented between review-kernel.md (where it actually lives) and the new llm-framework.md section — undermining the 'single authoritative reference' goal.
If the intent is to add a cross-reference section to llm-framework.md rather than move the authoritative four-pass content there, the plan should say so explicitly.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the rejected-alternative justification to 'the four-pass contract already lives in docs/review-kernel.md, and llm-framework.md is the appropriate home for a project-criteria runtime contract section because…' (supply the real reason), or add the section to review-kernel.md where the four-pass contract is actually documented.

findings.finding: The AC verification script checks for the literal marker token `round_a` (underscore) within the new section body, but the plan's own description of that aspect uses the phrase 'Round A/B fan-in + logical id'. An implementer following the plan's natural-language aspect descriptions will naturally write 'Round A' or 'Round-A' (not `round_a`), producing a correctly-documented section that nonetheless fails the automated marker check. The plan never tells the author to embed these exact lowercase-underscore tokens verbatim — the markers are only visible in the AC shell command.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — first AC bullet (the rtk shell command) vs Approach section aspect 1 description
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note under the Approach section (or inline with each aspect description) listing the exact marker string each aspect must contain, e.g.: 'Aspect 1 must include the token `round_a` and the phrase `logical criterion id`.' Alternatively, align the AC markers to the plan's own phrasing: replace `round_a` with `round a` and `logical criterion id` with `logical id`.

findings.finding: The Approach section requires four substantive content areas (Round A/B fan-in, repository-effective routing, criteria eval path, configuration-error behavior), but the AC only verifies presence of five token strings — a one-sentence stub containing all five strings satisfies the AC without documenting any of the four content areas meaningfully. There is a gap between 'what we'll do' and 'what done looks like'.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (single AC) vs. Approach section bullet list
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the AC assertion to also check for one representative phrase from each of the four Approach bullets, e.g. add `'fan-in'` (Round A/B), `'repository-effective'` (routing), `'resolution'` or `'admission'` (criteria eval path), and `'prompt/eval spec is missing'` (config-error behavior) to the `all(x in s ...)` list.

findings.finding: The plan body consistently describes 'four end-to-end contract aspects' and says 'all four aspects', but the verification command checks five distinct literal markers (['round_a','logical criterion id','effective routing','criteria eval','configuration error']) and the AC description explicitly says 'five aspect markers'. An executor who structures the new section around four headings may naturally roll 'round_a' and 'logical criterion id' into a single paragraph without including both strings verbatim, causing the check to fail. The plan never states that aspect 1 requires two separate literal markers.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — first AC bullet; plan body 'four end-to-end contract aspects' and 'all four aspects'
findings.suggested_fix:
In the plan body, replace 'four end-to-end contract aspects' with 'five verifiable aspects' and split Aspect 1 into two entries:
1. **Round A/B fan-in** — … (must include the literal token `round_a`)
2. **Logical criterion id** — … (must include the literal token `logical criterion id`)
Then update the second AC bullet's parenthetical list from '(fan-in, Pass-3 routing, eval resolution, configuration error)' to '(round_a fan-in, logical criterion id, effective routing, criteria eval, configuration error)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove or increase the `[:5000]` truncation in the AC2 proving command, or replace it with a check that scans the full section body, so the marker check cannot produce a false negative on a valid document.
evidence: "sec=s.split('Code-review project criteria',1)[1][:5000]"
The five markers include 'configuration error', which corresponds to the fourth (last) subheading; a verbose section could push it beyond 5000 chars.
findings.finding: The AC2 proving command truncates the section body to the first 5000 characters (`[:5000]`), meaning if the 'configuration error' marker (the last listed) appears after that byte offset — possible if the section is written verbosely — the command returns a false negative (reports a missing marker that is actually present). This makes the proving command unreliable as the section grows.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — second bullet (AC2 Python one-liner)
scenarios: Author writes a thorough section (>5000 chars) where 'configuration error' marker appears after the truncation boundary — AC2 falsely fails despite the marker being present.
A future editor adds prose between subheadings, inadvertently pushing markers past the cutation boundary, causing CI to fail on a valid doc.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `sec=s.split('Code-review project criteria',1)[1][:5000]` with `sec=s.split('Code-review project criteria',1)[1]` — the full tail of the file after the section heading is already scoped to the section's content for this purpose, and the marker strings are specific enough that cross-section false-positives are not a realistic concern.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
